title: Slice 3: /router cost + /router cleanup commands + sign-off
description:
### Description
Build CLI commands for historical usage review and data pruning, then manually verify everything works end-to-end in pi.

### Tasks
1. **`/router cost [router_name] [--since 24h|1w|1m|2m|all]`**
   - Query `queryUsage()` with optional filters
   - Format table: router name, model ref, input tokens, output tokens, total tokens, total cost, timestamp
   - Show aggregated totals at bottom (total_tokens, total_cost)
   - Default `--since`: `all`
   - Group by router name when no `router_name` provided
2. **`/router cleanup 24h|1w|1m|2m|all`**
   - Parse interval argument
   - Call `cleanupUsage()` with computed timestamp threshold
   - Return deleted row count
3. **Manual sign-off in pi**
   - Run router extension in pi and verify:
     - Footer shows real cost after delegation
     - `/router cost` queries and displays usage
     - `/router cleanup` prunes old records

### Dependencies
Blocked by Slice 1 (needs queryUsage/cleanupUsage functions) and Slice 2 (footer display)

### Reference
PRD: `docs/prd-cost-usage.md` → "Commands" section

### Labels
ready-for-human
close_reason: implemented+verified: /router cost + /router cleanup merged to master

title: Slice 1: SQLite usage table + capture at 3 points
description:
### Description
Add SQLite usage table and capture usage at 3 points in `provider.ts`.

### Tasks
1. Export `getDb()` from `rate-limit-tracker.ts` (or create shared db module)
2. Create `extensions/usage-tracker.ts` with:
   - `recordUsage()`, `queryUsage()`, `cleanupUsage()`, `_setDbForTesting()`
   - Reuses `getDb()` singleton
   - Creates `usage` table + indexes on init
3. Capture at 3 points in `provider.ts`:
   - **Success** (`tryModel`, `event.type === "done"`): capture `delegatedStream.result().usage` → call `recordUsage()`
   - **Error-after-content**: capture partial usage from stream result → call `recordUsage()`
   - **Error-before-content** (`routeStream` catch): attempt `delegatedStream.result().usage` if available → call `recordUsage()`
4. Add `CLEANUP_INTERVALS` and `USAGE_TABLE_NAME` constants to `constants.ts`
5. Wrap all usage capture in try-catch (non-blocking, fire-and-forget)
6. Handle undefined/null usage fields with defaults (0)
7. Write `usage-tracker.test.ts` with hermetic tests (in-memory DB)

### Reference
PRD: `docs/prd-cost-usage.md`
Prior art: `rate-limit-tracker.ts`, `rate-limit-tracker.test.ts`, `provider.ts`, `provider.test.ts`, `constants.ts`

### Labels
ready-for-agent

title: Slice 2: Footer cost auto-accumulation
description:
### Description
Copy actual usage from delegated stream result to `output.usage` before `stream.end()` so pi automatically shows real costs in the footer.

### Tasks
1. In `provider.ts`, in `tryModel` when `event.type === "done"`:
   - Before `stream.end()`, set `output.usage = delegatedStream.result().usage`
2. In error-after-content path:
   - Before `stream.end()`, set `output.usage = delegatedStream.result().usage` (partial usage)
3. Error-before-content path: do NOT set `output.usage` (no content produced)
4. Verify that pi footer accumulates costs correctly (integration)

### Dependencies
Blocked by Slice 1 (needs usage capture infrastructure in place)

### Reference
PRD: `docs/prd-cost-usage.md` → "Footer Cost Display" section

### Labels
ready-for-agent

title: MaxTokens sync + integrasi sign-off
description:
## What to build

Pastikan maxTokens juga ikut update sesuai model aktif setelah fallback. Hit manual sign-off.

### Detail
- Setelah Slice 1-3 selesai, verifikasi maxTokens di footer/session juga sesuai model aktif
- Kalo perlu, tambah mutation ctx.model.maxTokens bareng ctx.model.contextWindow di syncContextWindow()

### Acceptance criteria
- [ ] maxTokens di session metadata sesuai model aktif setelah fallback
- [ ] Manual test: prompt panjang via router → gak kena 413/overflow
- [ ] Test pindah session → CTW + maxTokens masih sesuai

## Blocked by
- pi-model-router-d1h
- pi-model-router-67b
- pi-model-router-ua2

title: CTW update di model_select event handler
description:
## What to build

Saat user ganti model via /model atau Ctrl+P ke router model, set ctx.model.contextWindow dari model aktif pertama (non-cooldowned) di fallback chain.

### Mutation points
- `extensions/index.ts` — `model_select` event handler yang udah ada
- Cek apakah model yg dipilih adalah router model (provider === PROVIDER_NAME)
- Kalo iya, cari fallback pertama yg gak cooldown, update CTW

### Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Pas ganti ke router model, CTW footer langsung sesuai model aktif pertama
- [ ] Pas ganti ke non-router model, CTW footer normal (gak diubah)
- [ ] Semua test existing masih passing

title: CTW update di tryModel error-after-content
description:
## What to build

Saat tryModel mendeteksi error setelah content diterima (erredAfterContent=true), setelah markRateLimited + return false, update ctx.model.contextWindow ke CTW model fallback pertama yang tidak dalam cooldown.

### Mutation points
- `extensions/provider.ts` — `tryModel`, setelah erredAfterContent flow
- Panggil helper `syncContextWindow()` yang sama dari Slice 1

### Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Setelah error-after-content, ctx.model.contextWindow berisi CTW model fallback berikutnya
- [ ] Gak ada side effect ke stream yang udah di-end
- [ ] Semua test existing masih passing

## Blocked by
- pi-model-router-d1h (CTW update di routeStream catch block)

title: CTW update di routeStream catch block
description:
## What to build

Saat routeStream catch block menangkap error model dan memanggil markRateLimited, update ctx.model.contextWindow ke CTW model fallback pertama yang tidak dalam cooldown.

### Mutation points
- `extensions/provider.ts` — `routeStream` catch block, setelah `markRateLimited`
- Helper `syncContextWindow()` untuk reusable lookup + mutasi ctx.model.contextWindow

### Data flow
1. Model_A ke error → markRateLimited(model_A)
2. Cari model berikutnya di chain yang gak cooldown (via isRateLimited)
3. Lookup CTW dari modelRegistry
4. ctx.model.contextWindow = ctw_baru

### Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Setelah model A error, ctx.model.contextWindow berisi CTW model B (fallback pertama)
- [ ] update silent — gak ada model_change notification ke user
- [ ] Semua test existing masih passing

title: Slice 3: Integrasi Practice / Sign-off (Escalating Cooldown)
description:
## Source PRD

`docs/prd-cooldown-escalation.md`

## What to build

Verifikasi manual bahwa escalating cooldown + SQLite persistence bekerja di pi. Setelah Slice 1 dan 2 diimplementasikan dan dideploy:

1. Reload router extension
2. Test dengan model yang sengaja di-rate-limit atau error — verify cooldown shared across sessions (multiple terminals)
3. Verify escalating tiers: 5 same-type errors → 1h cooldown, 7+ same-type errors → 6h cooldown
4. Verify counter resets on different error type or success
5. Verify `/router status` shows error type and consecutive count
6. Verify `/router clearcache` clears all cooldowns (including escalated ones)
7. Verify cooldown survives pi restart (SQLite persistence)
8. Verify no regression — healthy models still behave normally

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] User test langsung di pi dan konfirmasi expected behaviour tercapai
- [ ] Tidak ada regresi yang terdeteksi
- [ ] Cooldown shared between terminals (cross-session)
- [ ] Escalation visible via `/router status`
- [ ] `/router clearcache` resets everything

title: Slice 2: Escalating Cooldown dengan Error Classification
description:
## Source PRD

`docs/prd-cooldown-escalation.md` — bagian **Error Type Classification**, **Escalating Cooldown Tiers**, **Module Changes**.

## What to build

Add escalating cooldown tiers based on consecutive same-type errors, and classify errors into categories.

### Key changes

1. **`extensions/rate-limit-tracker.ts`** — Extend from Slice 1:
   - `isTransientError()` → `classifyError()` returning category string (`"rate_limit"`, `"server_error"`, `"timeout"`, `"auth"`, `"other"`)
   - Keep `isTransientError` as backward-compat wrapper
   - `markRateLimited(ref, cooldownMs?, errorType?)` — when `errorType` matches existing entry and cooldown still active, increment consecutive counter and apply escalating duration
   - Add `resetCooldown(ref)` — clears cooldown entry entirely
   - Callers in `provider.ts` use `classifyError()` and pass error type to `markRateLimited`
   - After successful model response, call `resetCooldown(ref)`

2. **`extensions/constants.ts`** — Add escalation tier constants:
   - `ESCALATION_TIER_1_MAX = 4`, `ESCALATION_TIER_2_MIN = 5`, `ESCALATION_TIER_2_MAX = 6`, `ESCALATION_TIER_3_MIN = 7`
   - `ESCALATION_COOLDOWN_TIER_1_MS = 300_000`, `TIER_2 = 3_600_000`, `TIER_3 = 21_600_000`

3. **`extensions/provider.ts`** — Update call sites:
   - Use `classifyError()` and pass error type
   - Call `resetCooldown(ref)` on success

4. **`extensions/commands.ts`** — Extend `/router status` to show error type and consecutive count

5. **Tests** — Add test suites:
   - Escalation: same-type 5x → 1h, 7x → 6h
   - Counter reset on different error type
   - Counter reset on success via `resetCooldown`
   - `resetCooldown` clears escalation
   - Provider-level cooldown uses `"provider_outage"` and never escalates

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `classifyError()` returns correct category for each error pattern
- [ ] 5 consecutive same-type errors → 1 hour cooldown
- [ ] 7 consecutive same-type errors → 6 hour cooldown (cap)
- [ ] Different error types → counter resets, stays at 5m tier
- [ ] Success after cooldown → `resetCooldown` clears escalation
- [ ] `/router status` shows error type and consecutive count
- [ ] Provider cooldown (`__provider:`) never escalates
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
notes:
## Design corrections (from review)

### Escalation: counter decoupled from expiry
The consecutive counter is NOT reset when cooldown expires. It only resets on:
- Different error type
- `resetCooldown(ref)` (model succeeded)
This ensures escalation works for spaced-out errors (e.g., user messages every 10 minutes).

### Cap consecutive at 12
Add upper cap: `consecutive <= 12`. Beyond 12 same-type errors, stay at max tier (6h). Prevents absurd durations from accumulated errors over days.

### `isTransientError` — remove entirely, not keep as wrapper
No backward-compat stub. No callers remain. Rename directly to `classifyError()`.

### Error-after-content race in tryModel
When error arrives after content + subsequent `done` event, `tryModel` must NOT return `true` (success). Add `erredAfterContent` flag. When `event.type === 'error'` with `contentReceived === true`, set flag. When `event.type === 'done'`, check flag: if set, `stream.end()` and return `false` (failure). This lets outer `routeStream` catch block handle cooldown uniformly.

### Provider-level cooldown limitation
Document that provider-level (`__provider:`) defaults to 5m and does NOT escalate. During 2-hour provider outage, every turn retries every 5 minutes. Future improvement: separate longer default for provider failures.

title: Slice 1: SQLite Backend untuk Cooldown Persistence
description:
## Source PRD

`docs/prd-cooldown-escalation.md` — bagian **SQLite Backend (replaces in-memory Map)** dan **Module Changes**.

## What to build

Replace the in-memory `Map` in `extensions/rate-limit-tracker.ts` with a local SQLite database (Node 22+ built-in `node:sqlite`).

### Key changes

1. **`extensions/rate-limit-tracker.ts`** — Major rewrite:
   - Replace `const RATE_LIMITED = new Map<string, number>()` with SQLite-backed storage via `node:sqlite`
   - Database location: `~/.local/share/pi/model-router.db` (respect `$XDG_DATA_HOME` when set)
   - Schema: `cooldowns` table with columns `model_ref TEXT PRIMARY KEY, error_type TEXT NOT NULL, expiry_at INTEGER NOT NULL, duration_ms INTEGER NOT NULL, consecutive INTEGER DEFAULT 1, updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL`
   - Lazy init: module-level singleton connection created on first operation
   - All public functions remain synchronous (`markRateLimited`, `isRateLimited`, `getActiveRateLimits`, `clearRateLimits`, `getRemainingCooldownMs`)
   - `clearRateLimits()` — deletes all rows from the table
   - Database created with `0o700` permissions

2. **`extensions/constants.ts`** — Add `XDG_DATA_HOME` resolution helper

3. **`extensions/rate-limit-tracker.test.ts`** — New test file with unit tests using `:memory:` SQLite

### Test seam

- `rate-limit-tracker.test.ts`: isolate with `:memory:` databases, one per `beforeEach`
- 57 existing tests must still pass unchanged

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All existing 57 tests pass without modification
- [ ] Cooldown state survives across module reloads (SQLite file persistence)
- [ ] `clearRateLimits()` deletes all rows
- [ ] `node:sqlite` used (no additional npm dependencies)
- [ ] Lazy init — no startup penalty if no cooldowns active
- [ ] Schema created with `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`
notes:
## Design corrections (from review)

### Schema
Remove `updated_at` column (dead, never used).
Add `CHECK(consecutive >= 1)` constraint.

### Connection init
Add these on first connection open:
- `PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL`
- `PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000`

### Testability
Export `_setDbForTesting(db?: DatabaseSync)` for injecting `:memory:` databases in tests. This is internal/test-only.

### Cross-session concurrency
Concurrent writes from multiple pi sessions are handled by `busy_timeout=5000` — write waits up to 5s before throwing. If throw occurs, cooldown not recorded → acceptable degradation (model retried next turn).

title: Integrasi practice / sign-off
description:
## Parent

Parent PRD: pi-model-router-6bw (Problem Statement), pi-model-router-ej1 (Testing Decisions)

## What to build

Verifikasi manual bahwa fix bekerja di pi. Setelah Slice 1 dan 2 diimplementasikan dan dideploy:

1. Reload router extension
2. Test dengan model yang sengaja rate-limited atau error
3. Verifikasi expected behaviour: model gagal di turn 1 langsung di-skip di turn 2, turn 3, seterusnya
4. Verifikasi `/router clearcache` mereset cooldown
5. Verifikasi tidak ada regresi — model healthy tetap dipakai

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] User test langsung di pi dan konfirmasi expected behaviour tercapai
- [ ] Tidak ada regresi yang terdeteksi

## Blocked by

- pi-model-router-olj (Cooldown untuk semua model error)
- pi-model-router-by6 (Cooldown untuk error-after-content)

title: Cooldown untuk error-after-content
description:
## Parent

Parent PRD: pi-model-router-6bw (Problem Statement), pi-model-router-35e (Implementation Decisions)

## What to build

Refactor `tryModel` di `provider.ts` agar error dari delegated stream yang terjadi SETELAH konten tetap dipropagasi ke outer catch untuk cooldown. Saat ini error-after-content hanya di-push ke stream tanpa trigger `markRateLimited`.

Perubahan di file: `extensions/provider.ts` — fungsi `tryModel`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Delegated stream yang mengirim `text_delta` lalu `error` → error dipropagasi ke outer catch → `markRateLimited(ref)` dipanggil
- [ ] Test: mock stream kirim `text_delta` → `error` event → `markRateLimited` dipanggil
- [ ] Test: dua sequential `streamSimple` calls → model dengan error-after-content di call pertama di-skip di call kedua
- [ ] Fallback notification tetap muncul di stream (user tetap lihat pesan fallback)

## Blocked by

- pi-model-router-olj (Cooldown untuk semua model error)
close_reason: implemented: commit e3edd12 — cooldown untuk error-after-content

title: Cooldown untuk semua model error
description:
## Parent

Parent PRD: pi-model-router-6bw (Problem Statement), pi-model-router-35e (Implementation Decisions)

## What to build

Hapus guard `isTransientError` di catch block `routeStream` di `provider.ts`. Saat ini `markRateLimited` hanya dipanggil kalau error message match `TRANSIENT_PATTERNS`. Ubah jadi SEMUA error (termasuk non-transient) trigger `markRateLimited`.

**Provider-level cooldown tetap gated** oleh `isProviderLevelError` — hanya error 502/503/504/sejenis yang trigger cooldown `__provider:{name}`.

Perubahan hanya di file: `extensions/provider.ts`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Catch block di `routeStream` memanggil `markRateLimited(ref)` untuk SEMUA error (tidak ada pengecualian selain `RouterAbortError`)
- [ ] `isProviderLevelError` masih dihormati — provider-level cooldown hanya untuk 502/503/504/sejenis
- [ ] Test: mock delegated `streamSimple` throw error dengan message yang tidak match `TRANSIENT_PATTERNS` → `markRateLimited(ref)` dipanggil
- [ ] Test: dua sequential `streamSimple` calls → model yang gagal di call pertama di-skip di call kedua

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately

title: Out of Scope
description:
- **Persistent cooldown across pi restarts**: Cooldown is in-memory only (Map). This PRD does not add persistence. On restart, all models are fresh.
- **Per-model cooldown duration config**: Uses a single `rateLimitCooldownMs` for all models. Per-model cooldown config is future work.
- **Separate "permanent error" blacklist**: Non-transient errors currently get the same cooldown as transient errors. A separate permanent blacklist (models that should never be retried) is out of scope.
- **Heuristic routing**: No changes to the model selection algorithm beyond cooldown-based skipping.
- **TUI changes**: No changes to UI components.

title: User Stories
description:
1. As a pi user with a router worker of 7 models where models 1-3 are consistently failing, I want those failed models to be skipped entirely on subsequent turns, so that I don't waste 9+ seconds per turn waiting for guaranteed failures.
2. As a pi user experiencing rate limits, I want rate-limited models to enter cooldown and be skipped on subsequent turns, so that I never retry a rate-limited model before its cooldown expires.
3. As a pi user whose model errors with a non-standard error message (e.g. "The provider encountered an issue"), I want that model to still be marked for cooldown and skipped on future turns, so that even errors with non-matching message patterns don't cause infinite retry loops.
4. As a pi user whose model streams partial content then hits a rate limit mid-response, I want that model to still be marked for cooldown on future turns, so that error-after-content scenarios don't silently bypass the cooldown system.
5. As a pi user whose temporarily-broken model recovers after some time, I want cooldowns to naturally expire and re-enable the model, so that recovered models are automatically tried again without manual intervention.
6. As a pi user who wants to force-reset all cooldowns (e.g. after adding a new API key), I want `/router clearcache` to clear all error states, so that I can manually reset the cooldown system.
7. As a pi user, I want the fallback notification message to clearly distinguish between "rate-limited (cooldown)" and "error (cooldown)" and "error (permanent)", so that I understand why specific models are being skipped.
8. As a pi user whose provider-wide outage occurs (502/503), I want the entire provider's models to be cooldowned, so that I don't retry each model of a dead provider individually.
9. As a developer maintaining the router extension, I want the cooldown logic to be testable via unit tests that verify `markRateLimited` is called for ALL error types, so that the fix is regression-proof.
10. As a developer maintaining the router extension, I want the `isRateLimited` check to correctly skip cooldowned models across multiple `streamSimple` calls, so that the "skip on next turn" behaviour works reliably.

title: Further Notes
description:
- The `isTransientError` function in `rate-limit-tracker.ts` continues to exist and may be useful for telemetry/logging even though it no longer gates cooldown application.
- Provider-level cooldown (`__provider:{name}`) correctly checks `isProviderLevelError` — only 502/503/504-like errors should cooldown the entire provider. Individual model errors should only cooldown that specific model.
- The existing "abort" guard (`RouterAbortError`) correctly does NOT trigger cooldown — pi-level timeouts are not model failures.
- After implementing, run `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` to verify no regressions.

title: Problem Statement
description:
The router extension iterates through a fallback chain of models on every turn (`streamSimple` call). When a model errors or hits a rate limit, the error state is not always recorded in the cooldown map (`RATE_LIMITED`). This causes two problems:
1. **Permanent retry loop**: Models that fail with non-transient errors (e.g. model not found, generic upstream error) are never marked for cooldown. They retry on every turn, burn time, and fail again — wasting 10-30 seconds per turn before reaching a working model.
2. **Error-after-content misses cooldown**: If a model streams partial content then errors (e.g. rate-limited mid-response), the error event is forwarded to the user but `markRateLimited` is never called. On the next turn the same model is tried again from scratch.
**Current behaviour** (7 models, models 1-3 broken, model 4 working):
```
Turn 1:  model 1 (rate-limit, 3s) → model 2 (error, 3s) → model 3 (error, 3s) → model 4 ✓
Turn 2:  model 1 (rate-limit, 3s) → model 2 (error, 3s) → model 3 (error, 3s) → model 4 ✓
Turn 3:  model 1 (rate-limit, 3s) → model 2 (error, 3s) → model 3 (error, 3s) → model 4 ✓
...
```
Every turn wastes ~9s retrying models that are guaranteed to fail.
notes:
Slice 1 selesai: commit 0d7ea60 — cooldown untuk semua model error
Slice 2 selesai: commit e3edd12 — cooldown untuk error-after-content

title: Solution
description:
Make ALL failed models "invisible" on subsequent turns by applying cooldown to every error — not just "transient" errors matching a fixed keyword list. When a model fails for any reason (rate limit, transient server error, permanent error, error-after-content), it enters cooldown and is skipped on the next turn.
**Expected behaviour:**
```
Turn 1:  model 1 (rate-limit)                 → model 2 (error) → model 3 (error) → model 4 ✓
Turn 2:  model 4 ✓                              (1,2,3 invisible)
Turn 3:  model 4 ✓                              (1,2,3 invisible)
Turn 4:  model 4 (rate-limit)                  → model 5 ✓
Turn 5:  model 5 ✓                              (1,2,3,4 invisible)
```
- All failed models get cooldown → invisible on next turn
- Rate-limited models get cooldown (unchanged)
- Error models get cooldown (NEW — previously only transient errors)
- Error-after-content models get cooldown (NEW — previously never cooldowned)
- Cooldown expiry re-enables the model (if still broken, fails again and re-enters cooldown)
- `/router clearcache` resets all cooldowns manually
